---
title: "MLX vs GGUF: Gemma 4 26B-A4B-it on M5 Max"
author: "haxlys"
date: today
jupyter: python3
---

## Why this comparison

Apple's MLX and ggerganov's GGUF/llama.cpp are the two dominant local-inference
runtimes on Apple Silicon. They ship the same model in different binary formats.
This report measures the gap on a single hardware target (M5 Max 128GB) using
Google's `gemma-4-26B-A4B-it` — a Mixture-of-Experts model with 4B active
parameters out of 26B total.

The pair: `lmstudio-community/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-MLX-8bit` (8-bit MLX, 26GB) vs
`unsloth/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it-GGUF` Q8_0 (~27GB). The quantization levels are
intentionally matched so the comparison isolates the **runtime**, not the quantizer.

## Methodology

- **Hardware**: Apple M5 Max, 128GB unified memory, macOS 26.4
- **Runners**: `mlx_lm.generate --verbose` (Python 3.13) vs `llama-bench` (llama.cpp from Homebrew)
- **Memory**: BSD `/usr/bin/time -l` "maximum resident set size", reconciled with `mlx.metal.get_peak_memory()` for MLX
- **Scenarios**: prefill ∈ {256, 1024, 4096, 8192} × generation ∈ {128, 512}
- **Repetition**: 1 warmup (discarded) + 3 measured runs per scenario
- **Determinism**: `--temp 0.0`
- **Quality**: 20 multilingual prompts (KO/EN, reasoning/code/creative/edge), output divergence via `paraphrase-multilingual-mpnet-base-v2` embedding cosine similarity

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
import sys
from pathlib import Path
ROOT = Path("..").resolve()
sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / "src"))
from llm_bench.aggregate import aggregate_means, load_raw
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

raw = load_raw(ROOT / "results" / "raw")
means = aggregate_means(raw) if not raw.empty else pd.DataFrame()
```

## Results

::: {.callout-note}
Charts in this section render from `results/raw/*.json`. If you see an empty plot,
run `uv run python scripts/run_bench.py --gguf-model …` first.
:::

### Generation speed

```{python}
#| echo: false
if not means.empty:
    fig = px.bar(means, x="scenario", y="tg_tps_mean", color="fmt",
                 barmode="group", error_y="tg_tps_std",
                 color_discrete_map={"mlx": "#0a84ff", "gguf": "#34c759"},
                 labels={"tg_tps_mean": "TG tok/s"})
    fig.show()
else:
    print("(no data yet)")
```

### Prompt processing speed

```{python}
#| echo: false
if not means.empty:
    fig = px.bar(means, x="scenario", y="pp_tps_mean", color="fmt",
                 barmode="group", error_y="pp_tps_std",
                 color_discrete_map={"mlx": "#0a84ff", "gguf": "#34c759"},
                 labels={"pp_tps_mean": "PP tok/s"})
    fig.show()
```

### Speed vs Memory (Pareto)

```{python}
#| echo: false
if not means.empty:
    fig = px.scatter(means, x="peak_mem_gb_mean", y="tg_tps_mean",
                     color="fmt", symbol="scenario",
                     color_discrete_map={"mlx": "#0a84ff", "gguf": "#34c759"})
    fig.show()
```

## Discussion

(Fill in after measurements: which runtime wins on PP vs TG, memory headroom,
cost per token, whether quality diverges meaningfully.)

## Limitations

- Single hardware target (M5 Max only)
- Single model architecture (MoE 26B; Dense models may show different patterns)
- Two quantization levels (8bit). 4-bit class is out of scope for v0.1.
- Quality compared via embedding similarity, not absolute score (no LLM judge)
- Concurrency, batched inference, KV-cache reuse not measured

## References

- [LLMCheck Apple Silicon LLM benchmarks](https://llmcheck.net/benchmarks)
- [llama.cpp llama-bench](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/tree/master/tools/llama-bench)
- [mlx-lm](https://github.com/ml-explore/mlx-lm)
